mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous monthly increase in real personal spending and housing starts signals broad-based consumer and capital demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 5-day returns with accuracy exceeding 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects robust real output that offsets higher discount rates, yielding positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods are followed by negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter policy, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: High inflation months show positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, refuting the tightening expectation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 200,000 confirm economic momentum and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS values above 200,000 predict negative 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent during a post-shock recovery phase sustains earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0 percent is followed by negative 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates productive capacity expansion without labor market overheating, predicting positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO and UNRATE divergence signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
